concept.title: API Documentation Overview
concept.shortdesc: Postman generates API documentation automatically from collections and lets you publish it to a public or private URL. Documentation stays synchronized with your collection as you update requests.

conbody.p: Postman documentation is built from the descriptions you write for each collection, folder, and request. Postman renders these descriptions as formatted HTML pages that include request details, parameters, headers, body schemas, and example responses.
Published documentation provides developers with a self-service reference for consuming your API. It includes interactive examples that readers can copy directly into their own environments.

section.title: Documentation Sources
section.p: Postman assembles documentation from the following sources in your collection:

ul.li: Collection description — An introduction and overview of the API, written in Markdown. This appears at the top of the published documentation page.
Folder descriptions — Introductions to logical groups of endpoints within the collection, such as Authentication or User Management.
Request descriptions — Detailed explanations of individual endpoints, including purpose, required parameters, and expected responses.
Parameter and header descriptions — Inline descriptions for each query parameter, path variable, and header.
Example responses — Saved example responses that Postman displays alongside each request in the documentation.

section.title: Documentation Visibility Options
section.p: When you publish documentation in Postman, you can choose one of the following visibility settings:

dlentry.dd: Anyone with the URL can view the documentation without signing in to Postman. Public documentation is indexed by search engines. Use this setting for open APIs or developer portals.
Only members of your Postman team can view the documentation. Use this setting for internal APIs that should not be publicly accessible.

note.p: Custom domains for published documentation require a Basic plan or higher.

section.title: Documentation Workflow
section.p: The recommended workflow for creating and publishing API documentation in Postman is:

ol.li: Write descriptions for the collection, folders, and each request using Markdown.
Add saved example responses to each request to illustrate expected outputs.
Generate a preview of the documentation from the collection menu.
Publish the documentation and configure the visibility and URL settings.
Optionally, customize the published documentation with a custom domain, logo, and color scheme.
